notes: A long multi-turn agentic pi session — FOUR back-to-back user prompts in ONE live pi REPL, several of which elicit auto-executed bash tool calls (create a file, list it, grep it, read it back) — stress-testing that irrlicht's transcript parser stays coherent across a large volume of interleaved assistant messages and tool-call/result pairs: clean ready→working→ready arcs on ONE rollout session_id, NO within-turn flicker, NO spurious waiting, session never wedged, torn down exactly once. WHY interactive script mode (not headless `pi --print`): headless pi is single-shot and exits with process_exited after one reply (the basic-turn / session-end shape), so it can't build a long multi-turn rollout; the pi interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout REPL alive across all four turns via send+wait_turn and kills the tmux session once at the end → a SINGLE process_exited, not four. Pi auto-executes read-only/bash tools with no permission gate (unlike modern claudecode defaults that stall paid-model auto-mode at the permission prompt), so the tool-call turns don't wedge in waiting — the tool calls the claudecode sibling had to drop are kept here. WHY the 3s sleeps between wait_turn and the next send: they sit past the classifier's debounce window so each turn's working→ready flip is observed cleanly before the next prompt re-enters working; without them the next send can land before the prior turn settles and the arcs blur. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the brief gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript file appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session (same shape as the pi/multi-turn-conversation and pi/token-accounting cells). The TURN ARCS, by contrast, must all land on the UUID rollout session: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); every later turn phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as, so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. COMPACTION-INTERLEAVE CAVEAT: if pi fires context compaction during the rollout the compaction summary interleaves as assistant content and may add an extra working→ready→working flicker that is NOT a user turn boundary. Compaction is not deterministic on a 4-turn proxy run, so the spec does NOT assert a compaction phase; it asserts the first FOUR user-turn arcs reset cleanly and never enter waiting, NOT an exact arc count. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, tool-result content (file names/contents the model picks), and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); pi may also emit MORE than four working→ready arcs if a single prompt produces multiple transcript-activity bursts or compaction fires — the spec asserts the first four arcs reset cleanly and never enter waiting, not an exact arc count. Stable structurally: at least four ready→working→ready arcs on one rollout session_id, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
First prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
First turn completes (file created via bash tool) → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Second prompt → working again on the same rollout session
Second turn completes (directory listed via bash tool) → back to ready
Third prompt → working again on the same rollout session
Third turn completes (file grepped via bash tool) → back to ready
Fourth prompt → working again on the same rollout session
Fourth turn completes (file read back via bash tool) → back to ready
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the fourth turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
